author: nc1234
content:
So on devnet 6 I notice there is a bug from `prysm-erigon-1` serving a invalid payload envelope via ExecutionPayloadEnvelopesByRange at slot 98912. It's supposed to have non-empty BAL but it serves a payload envelope with empty BAL and original signature. `lodestar-erigon-1` received this during sync, didn't pass the signature verification and it became stuck.
  I think this is due to Prysm not persisting BAL but calls EL for BAL and then do the envelope reconstruction to serve reqresp (and beacon API I think). In this case, I believe Erigon returns `pruned history unavailable` or something (can't verify because I can't call `engine_getPayloadBodiesByHashV2` to that node directly), Prysm just put emtpy BAL into the envelope. Nonetheless I think prysm should do a quick check on the envelope before they serve. <@&595683609080365088>

author: taratorio
content: So on devnet 6 I notice there is a bug
